mnemonics.title: Package Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: I Don't Skip Installations (IDSI): Init file -> Directory structure -> Setup.py -> Install with pip

mnemonics.title: Core IoT Package Concepts
mnemonics.content: Daring Detectives Catch Spies: Device Management -> Data Processing -> Communication Protocols -> Security

mnemonics.title: Package Structure Elements
mnemonics.content: ISMS: __Init__.py -> Setup.py -> Modules -> Subpackages. Remember: 'I Start Making Software'

mnemonics.title: Virtual Environment Benefits
mnemonics.content: CAGE: Conflict Avoidance -> App Isolation -> Global protection -> Environment replication

acronyms.usage: Primary tool for installing Python packages from PyPI
Isolated Python environment for project-specific dependencies
Hardware interface package for IoT device communication

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a golden key - this file 'unlocks' a directory as a Python package
Visualize nested Russian dolls - each layer represents subpackage/module levels
Picture a protective bubble around your project isolating it from system-wide packages
See MQTT as bicycle couriers (lightweight), HTTP as trucks (heavy payloads)
Imagine a spider web connecting packages - cut one thread (dependency) and the web collapses

analogies.analogy: Like a toolbox - each tool (module) has specific functions, organized in compartments (subpackages)
Similar to a lunchbox - keeps your project's dependencies separate from others' meals (system packages)
Like a hotel reception - registers new guests (devices), manages their stays (configurations), and checks them out (retires)
Like recipe ingredients - miss one (dependency) and the dish (application) won't work properly
